text_blocks.snippet: If you are experiencing high levels of stress in Vancouver, there are numerous, largely free resources available, ranging from immediate crisis support to long-term counselling and self-help programs.
Here is a guide to finding help with stress in Vancouver and across BC:
If you are in distress or urgent need of someone to talk to:

list.snippet: 9-8-8: Call or text this number to reach a trained responder (Canada-wide).
310-Mental Health Support: Call 310-6789 (no area code needed) for 24/7, free, and confidential emotional support, information, and resources.
1-800-SUICIDE: Call 1-800-784-2433 if you are experiencing feelings of distress or despair.
Vancouver Access and Assessment Centre (AAC): For non-emergency but urgent help, residents can call 604-675-3700 or visit in person, available 7:30 am to 9:30 pm (walk-in) or 10:00 pm (phone).
Canadian Mental Health Association (CMHA) - North and West Vancouver: Offers short-term, low-cost counseling.
Family Services of the North Shore: Provides free counseling support for up to 6 weeks.
REACH Medical Clinic: Offers free virtual counselling sessions covered by MSP.
Moving Forward Family Services: Offers virtual counselling, including for those with limited income or no insurance.
Watari Counselling and Support Services: Provides free, confidential counselling in the Downtown Eastside/Strathcona area.
BounceBack BC: A free, evidence-based program offering online, video, and phone-based coaching to manage stress, anxiety, and low mood.
YMCA Y Mind: A free program for youth (13–30) and adults (31+) to learn how to manage stress and anxiety.
MindShift™ CBT: An app that uses scientifically proven cognitive behavioural therapy strategies to manage anxiety.
Living Life to the Full: An 8-week course that teaches practical skills for coping with stress and boosting mood.
Indigenous People: KUU-US Crisis Response Service (1-800-588-8717, 24/7) or Hope for Wellness (1-855-242-3310).
Students: Here2Talk offers 24/7, free, confidential counseling for post-secondary students.
Young People (12–24): Foundry BC provides free, confidential online or in-person support.
Healthcare Workers: Care to Speak offers peer support for BC healthcare workers.
Call 8-1-1: Speak with a nurse or health service navigator 24/7 for advice and to find services in your community.
Visit a Walk-in Clinic: A family doctor or nurse practitioner can provide referrals and support.
Use "Help Starts Here": Visit HelpStartsHere.gov.bc.ca to find over 2,600 listings of mental health and substance use resources in BC.

text_blocks.snippet: If you are in immediate danger of harming yourself or others, please call 9-1-1 or visit the nearest emergency department.
